meta.question: How many alerts were escalated to compliance in 2026 Q2?

response.answer: To answer your question regarding the number of alerts escalated to compliance in 2026 Q2, we can refer to Source [1]. According to this document, in 2026 Q2, there were 121 alerts that were escalated to compliance. [1]

contexts.text:
# AML Monitoring — Quarterly Desk Metrics (2026 Q2)

Aggregate, de-identified monitoring statistics for the commercial portfolio. No
single-customer case detail appears in this note (analyst clearance).

| Metric | 2026 Q1 | 2026 Q2 |
|---|---|---|
| Alerts generated | 1,284 | 1,412 |
| Alerts escalated to compliance | 96 | 121 |
| Accounts under enhanced monitoring | 38 | 41 |
| Median alert disposition time (days) | 6.2 | 5.4 |

## Commentary
Escalations rose 26% quarter-over-quarter, driven primarily by cash-structuring
typologies in the cash-intensive trade segment. High-risk accounts continue to
account for a disproportionate share of escalations. Specific account
identifiers and case narratives are restricted to compliance reviewers.

# Atlas Bank — Anti-Money-Laundering (AML) Program Policy

## Purpose
This policy establishes Atlas Bank's program to detect, prevent, and report money
laundering and terrorist financing in accordance with the Bank Secrecy Act (BSA)
and applicable regulations. It applies to all employees, contractors, and lines
of business.

## Key reporting thresholds
- **Currency Transaction Report (CTR):** filed for cash transactions exceeding
  **$10,000** in aggregate by or on behalf of one person in a single business day.
- **Suspicious Activity Report (SAR):** filed when the bank knows, suspects, or has
  reason to suspect that a transaction (or pattern) aggregating **$5,000 or more**
  involves funds from illegal activity, is designed to evade BSA requirements
  (including **structuring** to avoid the CTR threshold), or has no apparent lawful
  purpose. SARs must be filed within **30 calendar days** of initial detection.

## Roles
- **Analysts** monitor alerts and document dispositions.
- **Compliance officers** review exceptions, approve SAR filings, and own the case
  record.
- The **BSA Officer** has final authority over the program.

## Confidentiality
The existence or contents of a SAR, and any supporting investigative material,
are strictly confidential and may not be disclosed outside authorized personnel.

3M Company — MD&A, Financial Condition (Form 10-Q, Q2 2023)

In November 2018, 3M's Board of Directors replaced the Company's February 2016
repurchase program with a new repurchase program. This new program authorizes the
repurchase of up to $10 billion of 3M's outstanding common stock, with no
pre-established end date. In the first six months of 2023, the Company purchased
$29 million of its own stock, compared to $773 million of stock purchases in the
first six months of 2022. As of June 30, 2023, approximately $4.2 billion
remained available under the authorization.

In February 2023, 3M's Board of Directors declared a first-quarter 2023 dividend
of $1.50 per share, an increase of 1 percent. This marked the 65th consecutive
year of dividend increases for 3M. In May 2023, 3M's Board of Directors declared
a second-quarter 2023 dividend of $1.50 per share.

# BSA/AML Regulatory Reference (Public Summary)

This non-confidential reference summarizes the principal U.S. anti-money-laundering
obligations relevant to Atlas Bank staff. It contains no customer information.

## Core obligations
- **Customer Identification Program (CIP):** verify the identity of each customer
  at account opening.
- **Customer Due Diligence (CDD):** understand the nature and purpose of customer
  relationships and develop a risk profile.
- **Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD):** apply heightened scrutiny to higher-risk
  customers, including those with complex ownership or exposure to high-risk
  jurisdictions.
- **Ongoing monitoring:** detect and report suspicious activity.

## Structuring
"Structuring" means breaking a large transaction into smaller amounts to evade the
$10,000 CTR reporting threshold. Structuring is itself a federal offense regardless
of whether the underlying funds are legitimate.

## Recordkeeping
Records supporting CTRs and SARs must be retained for **five years**.

American Express Company — MD&A, Business Environment (FY2022)

For 2022, we reported net income of $7.5 billion, or $9.85 per share, compared
with net income of $8.1 billion, or $10.02 per share, a year ago. The reduction
in net income reflected credit reserve builds and net losses in our Amex Ventures
strategic investment portfolio in the current year compared with sizeable credit
reserve releases and significant net gains in our Amex Ventures strategic
investment portfolio in the prior year.

Worldwide network volumes for the year increased 21 percent compared to the prior
year (24 percent on an FX-adjusted basis). Total revenues net of interest expense
increased 25 percent year-over-year. Discount revenue, our largest revenue line,
increased 25 percent year-over-year. Net card fees increased 17 percent
year-over-year, as new card acquisitions reached record levels in 2022 and Card
Member retention remained high, demonstrating the impact of investments we have
made in our premium value propositions.

Card Member loans increased 22 percent year-over-year, with the majority of
growth coming from existing Card Members. Provisions for credit losses increased
versus the prior year, reflecting a reserve build of $617 million compared with a
reserve release of $2.5 billion in the prior year.

3M Company — MD&A, Results of Operations (FY2022 vs FY2021)

Operating Expenses (Percent of net sales)        2022     2021    Change
Cost of sales                                    56.2%    53.2%     3.0%
Selling, general and administrative (SG&A)       26.5     20.4      6.1
Research, development and related (R&D)            5.4      5.6     (0.2)
Gain on business divestitures                    (8.0)       --     (8.0)
Goodwill impairment expense                       0.8        --      0.8
Operating income margin                          19.1%    20.8%    (1.7)%

Selling, General and Administrative Expenses: SG&A, measured as a percent of
sales, increased in 2022 when compared to the same period last year. SG&A was
impacted by increased special item costs for significant litigation primarily
related to steps toward resolving Combat Arms Earplugs litigation (discussed in
Note 16) resulting in a 2022 second quarter pre-tax charge of approximately
$1.2 billion, certain impairment costs related to exiting PFAS manufacturing
(see Note 15), costs related to exiting Russia (see Note 15), divestiture-related
restructuring charges (see Note 5), and continued investment in key growth
initiatives. These increases were partially offset by restructuring benefits and
ongoing general 3M cost management.

Cost of Sales increases were primarily due to 2022 special item costs for
significant litigation from additional commitments to address PFAS-related
matters at 3M's Zwijndrecht, Belgium site (discussed in Note 16), higher raw
materials and logistics costs.

Gain on Business Divestitures: In the third quarter of 2022, 3M recorded a
pre-tax gain of $2.7 billion related to the split-off and combination of its
Food Safety business with Neogen Corporation.
